name: Kohler
description: Visual QC starter pack based on the supplied Kohler QC guidelines and sample QC report.

rules.title: Text is readable immediately
rules.instruction: All text must be clearly readable at first glance, without needing effort or zoom.

rules.title: Copy has strong contrast
rules.instruction: Copy must maintain strong contrast from the background and avoid grey-on-grey or low-contrast combinations.

rules.title: Copy avoids product edges and reflections
rules.instruction: Do not place copy on hero product edges, object boundaries, or high-contrast reflections that make the text feel merged or unstable.

rules.title: Hero product remains the focal point
rules.instruction: The hero product should remain clear, prominent, and unobstructed, without awkward crops reducing product comprehension.

rules.title: No slivers or edge breaks
rules.instruction: Background and artwork must extend fully to the canvas edge with no slivers, thin gaps, ghost borders, or floating edge textures.

rules.title: Distracting reflections are removed
rules.instruction: Reflections, mirror patches, and bright distractions should not compete with the product, logo, or copy.

rules.title: Safe areas are respected
rules.instruction: Copy and logo must sit inside safe-area guides for the target format and should not feel cramped against trim edges.

rules.title: Layout works across target sizes
rules.instruction: The composition should still work in each intended size without crowding, broken balance, or unsafe cropping.

rules.title: Visual hierarchy remains clear
rules.instruction: Maintain a clear hierarchy between logo, hero product, and copy; the user should understand the frame in one glance.

rules.title: Layout feels balanced
rules.instruction: Elements should feel intentionally distributed across the frame, without awkward clustering in the same vertical or horizontal zone.

rules.title: Logo stays distinct and uncontested
rules.instruction: The Kohler logo must remain crisp, unobstructed, and free from nearby visual elements that mimic or conflict with its shape.

rules.title: Gradient improves readability naturally
rules.instruction: If a gradient is used behind copy, it should improve readability, extend naturally to the edge, and avoid abrupt cuts or visible banding.

rules.title: Gradient does not create artificial spotlighting
rules.instruction: Avoid hard gradient lines, radial spotlight effects, or abrupt overlays that visibly cut across the product.

rules.title: Typography is consistent with benchmark
rules.instruction: Typography should feel consistent with approved Kohler static benchmarks, including perceived font size and hierarchy.

rules.title: Line breaks are clean
rules.instruction: Avoid single-word lines or awkward line breaks that weaken readability or hierarchy.

name: Colpal EU
description: Visual QC starter pack based on the supplied Colpal EU checklist structure, including product integrity, typography, and localization checks.

rules.title: Product visual matches the intended variant
rules.instruction: Check that the product shown is the correct variant for the asset and that no wrong pack, flavor, or SKU cues appear.

rules.title: Variant cues stay internally consistent
rules.instruction: Packaging, color cues, product claims, and supporting visuals should all point to the same product variant without contradiction.

rules.title: Primary copy is highly legible
rules.instruction: Primary copy must remain highly legible against the background, with strong contrast and no visual competition from nearby assets.

rules.title: Text and icons have breathing room
rules.instruction: Text inside circles, badges, or icon containers should have comfortable inner padding and must not feel crowded.

rules.title: Icons and badges respect edge spacing
rules.instruction: Icons, circles, and bordered visual devices should keep even spacing from their container edge and must not feel clipped.

rules.title: Asterisks and superscripts are correctly handled
rules.instruction: Asterisks, superscripts, and footnote markers should be visible, properly aligned, and clearly associated with the relevant claim text.

rules.title: No awkward orphan or widow lines
rules.instruction: Avoid orphan and widow line breaks that make the composition feel unfinished or reduce reading flow.

rules.title: Type hierarchy stays consistent
rules.instruction: Headline, support copy, disclaimers, and any icon labels should maintain a clear and consistent hierarchy.

rules.title: Product and hero visuals are cleanly framed
rules.instruction: Key product visuals, pack shots, and hero imagery should be fully framed without awkward crop cuts or clipped focal details.

rules.title: Layout adapts across output sizes
rules.instruction: The composition should still work in alternate sizes without crowding, broken reading order, or unsafe placement.

rules.title: Logo integrity is maintained
rules.instruction: The brand logo must be crisp, unobstructed, correctly colored, and visually distinct from surrounding elements.

rules.title: Localized asset matches the approved master
rules.instruction: Compare the asset visually to the approved English master and ensure structure, emphasis, and hierarchy remain aligned after localization.

rules.title: Cross-language consistency is preserved
rules.instruction: When adapted for another language, the asset should preserve the same intent, visual hierarchy, and key content relationships.

rules.title: Claims and supporting content look complete
rules.instruction: Claims, disclaimers, legal markers, and support text should appear complete, visible, and not obviously missing or truncated.
